originalAlarm.description: The value written to `&__pdata_0` is never used.

sanitizedDescription: The value written to `&pdata` is never used.
model:
(define-fun CONFIG_REGULATOR_MAX8660 () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_OF () Bool
  true)
reasoning: d6c7e113959.c L5: '*pdata++;' in max8660_pdata_from_dt — this post-increments the pointer pdata (equivalent to pdata++, discarding the read value). The incremented value of pdata (the local pointer copy) is never subsequently read (function returns 0 immediately). The dead store is of pdata's new value after the increment. The // ERROR comment at L18 in the caller refers to the caller-level bug; the dead store at L5 is a distinct new bug in this function. PC=CONFIG_REGULATOR_MAX8660=T AND CONFIG_OF=T correctly identifies the condition for this function to exist (inside nested #ifdef blocks at L1-2). No library macros. New bug, correctly constrained PC.
